name: Green Pepper-And-Tomato Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 large Onion, cut into 6ths and sliced thinly crosswise
2 pounds Ripe tomatoes; peeled, seeded and chopped
2 medium Green bell peppers, chopped into small squares
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound the garlic in a mortar with 1 teaspoon salt until it forms a paste.
2. Gradually mix in the chopped basil, parsley, thyme, paprika, and tomato paste.
3. Warm the olive oil in a soup pot, add the garlic-herb paste, and stir together.
4. Add the bay leaves and onion to the pot; sprinkle saffron over the onion.
5. Cook for 10 minutes or until the onion has softened.
6. Stir in the chopped green bell peppers, tomatoes (with their juice), and water.
7. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 25 minutes.
8. In a separate pot, bring 1½ cups of water to a boil; add a dash of salt and rice.
9. Cook until the rice is tender (about 12-15 minutes), then drain.
10. Stir the cooked rice into the soup, season with pepper.
11. Serve garnished with fresh herbs and cheese.
